Reconcile MCMC subject indices with subject IDs

Source: R/reconcile_subject_ids.R
reconcile_subject_ids.Rd

reconcile_subject_ids() joins unpacked MCMC output to the subject ID lookup table and resolves a single Subject identifier for every row.

  • Individual-level parameters match a row in ids, so they receive the original subject ID.

  • Population-level parameters (e.g., mu.par, prec.par, prec.logy) have no matching ID, so they retain the parameter name produced by unpack_jags() as their identifier. The temporary index columns are then dropped and the cleaned parameter names (Param) are promoted to Parameter for downstream use.

Usage

reconcile_subject_ids(jags_unpacked, ids)

Arguments

jags_unpacked

A tibble::tbl_df returned by unpack_jags() (after the Iso_type join), containing Subject, Subnum, Param, and Parameter columns.

ids

A tibble::tbl_df with a Subject_mcmc column (the subject ID) and a Subject column (the MCMC index as a character string).

Value

A tibble::tbl_df with a single resolved Subject column and a Parameter column holding the cleaned parameter name.

Author

Sam Schildhauer

On this page

Developed by Peter Teunis, Samuel Schildhauer, Kwan Ho Lee, Kristen Aiemjoy, Douglas Ezra Morrison.
